paragraphs.body: The similarities and differences between the Cathedral of Learning in Pittsburgh and Saint Mary’s Cathedral in Yangon highlights the diversity in architectural styles and building structures. The ways these buildings are used in different cultures emphasizes the purposes and intentions shaped by each society. Through these examples, one can see the goodwill expressed by each culture When I first imagined the Cathedral of Learning, the schools from Harry Potter came to mind. The architecture of CL resembles the medieval Gothic castles seen in Harry Potter. I pictured it would be composed of different schools under different names, similar to houses in Harry Potter. And I also expected that Pitts would design their merchandise to reflect the vibe of the Cathedral. For example, creating pens that assemble magical sticks and books with vintage covers. On analyzing the word “Cathedral”(the principal church of a bishop), I thought it was once a church for dioceses, bishops and christians to worship. However, it was never a church based on online sources. With the definition of Cathedral and interior design of Cathedral being majestic, I expected it to be a quiet place for students to read and study. Without considering the amount of students that Pitts have, I was naive about the idea that the CL will be a quiet study place. Upon arriving at the CL, I was one of many students trying to find my classroom on the first day of lecture. The building was bustling with activity—there were plenty of students coming and going, along with school tours and visitors admiring the CL’s elegance. Although it was crowded, it didn’t become noisy because of the high vaulted ceiling. Due to the crowd and the pressure of finding my classroom, I barely had time to notice the magnificent interior. After finishing my class, I finally took a moment to observe the architecture—the vaulted ceilings and the dim environment seemed contradictory to my understanding of a typical school building. Moreover, the interior structures of the pointed arches, intricate stone designs and big windows make it look more like an old medieval building. The decoration of the place matched exactly to what I imagined before visiting there. It is also impressive to see how each classroom door and the interior design of the rooms aligned with the architecture of the building. Because of these, I could hardly imagine it as a school building; it felt more like a historic building that needs to be preserved as a museum. Additionally, I also learned about the history of the Cathedral of Learning, which was built for educational purposes with funds from the students. The Cathedral symbolizes that there is no peak to learning(学无止尽), representing the hope to enhance their academic journey. CL将会一直提供给学生们学无止尽的旅途 Saint Mary’s Cathedral Church, located in Yangon, Myanmar, has a similar architectural structure to the Cathedral of Learning in Pittsburgh. While St Mary’s was completed in 1899 and the Cathedral of Learning in 1926, both buildings have rich histories. The church, built under British colonial rule, reflects its history and reminds people of the city’s rich religious and cultural heritage. In terms of the architectural style, Saint Mary’s church features a more vibrant use of color in its interior design, particularly with its stained glass windows, which create a sense of sacredness by allowing light to filter through in exquisite, colorful patterns. This feature enhances the atmosphere of worship. In contrast to the 42-story Cathedral of Learning, the lower vaulted ceiling of the St Mary fosters a more intimate, communal atmosphere, further strengthening the spiritual ambiance for Christian worshipers. Additionally, Saint Mary’s Cathedral Church, true to its purpose as a place of Christian worship, is mostly filled with rows of seats without tables. Whereas, the Cathedral of Learning is a place for students to learn and study. For me, a building like the Cathedral of Learning, with such a long history, should be preserved and maintained either as a museum or a sacred place for religious events or a convention center. In my culture, a construction like a Cathedral would not be built for the intent of a school. Historic buildings with refined architecture are often used as city hall, convention centers or museums in Myanmar. School buildings are generally just functional structures, not the kind of building as ancient or as grand as Cathedral. I find it hard to imagine a place with such ancient charm being used for academic and educational purposes. Nevertheless, the dedication of Cathedral of Learning reflects the founder’s good will, with the belief that the new generation can enlighten themselves through the transformative power of knowledge and learning. Similarly, the intent behind building St Mary’s Cathedral shares the same goodwill as the CL, instead it is orientated towards Christian believers. The presence of the church fosters a religious community that emphasizes the values of empathy, support and community welfare.

annotations.comment: CL - Please use the abbreviation more consistently throughout the essay.

paragraphs.body: Through these traditions, a stronger, more united community is formed.

The use of the Cathedral of Learning as a space for students broadens my perspective, showing that such a magnificent place can also function as a study environment. Both Saint Mary’s Cathedral and Cathedral of Learning carry the same word “Cathedral”, symbolizing places of significance, improvement and gathering whether for religious purposes or education goals. From both Cathedrals, one can see how different cultures have contributed their efforts and generosity towards their communities. These contributions ensure that future generations will continue to uphold these values of service and kindness, projecting them in meaningful ways.
